_comment: Physical-seed exemplar for BrickWallGenerator: common/American bond -- a header course (bricks turned so their short end shows, rotation_deg=90 in BrickWallGenerator's domain-specific reinterpretation of the generic rotation_deg field, see the generator's own docstring) inserted every 6 courses among otherwise running-bond stretcher courses, per notes/research/brick_street_construction_methods_2026_07_16.md sec8.1 (Brick Industry Association Technical Notes 30: 'a course of headers inserted every five or six courses... a header is always centered on a stretcher'). 6 was chosen as within the documented 5-6 course range. Header exposed width is a DERIVED constant = brick_length/2 = 0.1m (real 8in/4in = 2:1 ratio, sec8.2) -- BrickWallGenerator computes this itself, not stored redundantly here.
_verification: 7-member unit cell, 6 courses tall: courses 0/2/4 are single stretchers at u=0 (unoffset), courses 1/3 are single stretchers at u=brick_length/2 (the standard half-brick running-bond stagger), course 5 is TWO headers at u=0 and u=brick_length/2 (header pitch = brick_length/2, so 2 headers span exactly 1 stretcher-length with no gap, matching the 2:1 length:header-width ratio exactly). lattice_a=(brick_length,0) repeats each course horizontally at the stretcher pitch; lattice_b=(0, 6*brick_width) repeats the whole 6-course pattern vertically with zero horizontal shear, so course 6 of the next repeat lands back at the course-0 (unoffset) phase, continuing the running-bond alternation seamlessly across repeat boundaries.
